text: Continuous integration is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a central repository. Each merge triggers an automated build, which compiles the code and runs tests to check for errors. This process is managed by a CI pipeline, a sequence of automated steps that happens every time new code is pushed. By running tests regularly, teams can catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. This helps reduce bugs in production, saves time, and keeps the project stable. Overall, continuous integration encourages smoother collaboration and faster development cycles.
